message:
fix(net): DNS resolver fallback thread + WebSocket split lock consolidation

DNS resolver: introduces spawn_blocking_dns() helper that checks for a
current Cx with a blocking pool handle and falls back to
spawn_blocking_on_thread() when no pool is available. This ensures DNS
lookups and TCP connect never block the runtime worker thread even when
driven outside a full runtime context. New test verifies the fallback
path runs on a different thread.

WebSocket split: consolidates the read_more() method to perform the IO
read and buffer extension within a single lock scope inside poll_fn,
eliminating the intermediate unlock between reading into a temp buffer
and copying into the shared read_buf. The buffer reservation now uses
the actual bytes read (max(8192, n)) instead of a fixed pre-allocation,
reducing unnecessary capacity growth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
